paper_title: Application of quantum computing to a linear non-Gaussian acyclic model for novel medical knowledge discovery

data_advantages: collected from wide range of patients

causal_inference.description: determines direction of causal relationship and estimates causal effect
causal_inference.requirement: direction of causality must be known in advance

causal_discovery.importance: critical when direction of causality is uncertain

conventional_methods.limitation: cannot uniquely identify causal graph structure

assumptions: error variables are non-Gaussian and independent
each variable has a linear relationship

lingam_details.mechanism: uses independence of error variables to identify parameters determining causal graph structure
lingam_details.limitation: accuracy depends on independence measure used

kernel_method.advantage: high accuracy in determining independence and estimating causal structure
kernel_method.robustness: works well even when error variables are close to Gaussian or when data has outliers
kernel_method.improvement_needed: seeking kernels with better accuracy than Gaussian kernels

quantum_kernels.description: apply quantum computing to kernel methods
quantum_computing_concepts: qubits can take superpositions of 0 and 1

title: Application of quantum computing to a linear non-Gaussian acyclic model for novel medical knowledge discovery
